title: Raffi Sarkissian
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Raffi Sarkissian
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: My classes and research focus on all aspects of media––from representation to the business of media industries; from film studies to Tik Toks and podcasting. More specifically, I study LGBT representation and the participatory politics of everyday people using media for activism. I also study award shows as a network of industry businesses (from trade magazines to celebrities and the red carpet all the way to bloggers and #FilmTwitter discourse). The primary classes I teach in the Communication department are: Media Industries Critical Media Theory Film and Communication Media Research Methods LGBT Media Studies
education_entries: Ph D in Communication and Journalism, University of Southern California
MA in Cinematic Arts, University of Southern California
BA in Communication Studies and Sociology, University of California, Los Angeles
research_interests: My current research is divided into three areas: *LGBT media activism in early YouTube days *LGBT representation in media (from film/TV to streaming and social media) *(De)Constructing industry narratives for award shows (analyzing trade publications, podcasts, and awards bloggers)
teaching_interests: Media Industries Critical Media Theory Film and Communication Media Research Methods LGBT Media Studies
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2025). It Got Corporate: Consuming Queer/Trans Celebrity . Spectator. Volume, 45.2. Pages, 24-33.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2025). Bros, Billy Eichner and a box office bomb: A case study of the burden of representation . Queer Studies in Media and Popular Culture. Volume, 10. Issue, 1. Pages, 61-83.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2025). Organic Representation as a Critical Media Approach to Leadership Studies in Popular Culture . New Directions in Student Leadership. Volume, 2025. Issue, 185. Pages, 75-80.

Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2025). The Intersection of LGBTQ+ Identities and Popular Culture: Examining the Stigmas, Stories and Social Realities of Mediated Health Discourses . Routledge. Pages, 98-112.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2023). Zapping Storms: Camp Parody in LGBT Video Activism Online . International Journal of Communication. Volume, 17. Pages, 4424-4445.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2020). "To Hell with Dreams": Resisting Controlling Narratives Through Oscar Season . NYU Press. Pages, 68-76.
Other (2019). Participatory Politics in the Age of Crisis . Henry Jenkins: Confessions of an Aca-fan (Academic Blog).
